response: The internet originated from 1960s US defense research, with ARPANET demonstrating the first message in 1969. The adoption of TCP/IP in 1983 and Tim Berners-Lee’s invention of the World Wide Web in 1989 (introducing HTML, URLs, and HTTP) laid the foundation for modern use. Key milestones include the 1993 Mosaic browser, the dot-com boom and 2001 crash, the rise of social media in the mid-2000s, and the 2007 iPhone launch enabling mobile internet. Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people and underpins all aspects of modern life.
